title: NBA 2K27 cover stars revealed — Victor Wembanyama, Caitlin Clark, and Derrick Rose
body: TL;DR: 2K just made it official: Victor Wembanyama, Caitlin Clark, and Derrick Rose are your NBA 2K27 cover athletes , each repping a different edition of the game. NBA 2K27 hits PS5, Xbox Series X|S, PC, and Nintendo Switch 2 on Sept. 4, with early access starting Aug. 28 for Deluxe and Ultra buyers. Pre-orders are now live. Victor Wembanyama, Caitlin Clark, and Derrick Rose are your NBA 2K27 cover athletes , and each one is fronting a different edition of the game. Wembanyama, the San Antonio Spurs center and the NBA's first-ever unanimous Defensive Player of the Year, headlines the Standard Edition. Caitlin Clark, the Indiana Fever guard who's rewritten what's possible in the WNBA, becomes the first WNBA player to get her own global NBA 2K cover with the Deluxe Edition. And Chicago Bulls legend Derrick Rose, the youngest MVP in NBA history, closes out the trio on the limited-time Ultra Edition, marking his second cover after NBA 2K13 . What you'll be paying for each edition Standard Edition The straightforward option, and the only one you can grab on Switch 2 ( $69.99) Deluxe Edition Comes with 100,000 VC, MyCAREER skill, and Gatorade boosts, jerseys for both Wembanyama and Clark, a batch of MyTEAM content, plus early access ( $99.99 ) Ultra Edition Everything Deluxe offers, plus an extra 35,000 VC, a Season 1 Pro Pass, a Rose-branded hoodie, and guaranteed premium MyTEAM cards ( $149.99 digital-only on PS5, Xbox Series X|S, and PC) If you already own NBA 2K26 , pre-ordering the Ultra on the same platform will get you a 10% discount through Sept. 3. Credit: NBA 2K Credit

title: Astronomers think some asteroids have been comets all along. Heres why.
body: For nearly three decades, astronomers tracked a 1,250-foot-wide object relatively close to Earth that they thought was an asteroid . It looked like one. It followed the expected path. Telescopes saw no glowing cloud of gas or dust tail sweeping behind it. Then, the space rock, called 1998 SH2, drifted from its usual orbit that gravity alone predicted. Something was giving it a tiny push. Astronomers eventually realized that the culprit was likely gas escaping from its surface a faint version of the same process that powers a comet . When NASA -led researchers pointed some of the world's most powerful telescopes at 1998 SH2, they finally found the evidence: a barely visible cloud and a wispy tail. The object that astronomers had cataloged as an asteroid for years had finally given up its secret. It was a " dark comet " a weakly active snowball in disguise. The discovery doesn't just correct the label for one object. It suggests astronomers may need to rethink the definitions for asteroids and comets. For generations, scientists have separated these two groups by what they see: asteroids look like bare, rocky bodies, while comets reveal themselves through glowing tails that form when their ice turns into gas near the sun . But 1998 SH2 shows that some comets may be so faint that they masquerade as asteroids. That could mean the solar system is teeming with many more hidden comets than astronomers ever realized. title: Hisense’s new PX4 Pro projector turns your living room into a giant movie theater — buy it for under $4,000
body: TL;DR: Hisense s PX4 Pro 4K triple laser UST projector is now available for $3,997.97 at Amazon. The company has officially launched the Hisense PX4 Pro 4K Home Theater ultra-short-throw (UST) Projector, a premium projector designed to deliver a cinema-style setup without needing a dedicated theater room. It s now available at Hisense and Amazon , starting from $3,997.97. The PX4 Pro uses Hisense s TriChroma RGB laser technology, which uses separate red, green, and blue lasers to create richer, more accurate colors. That means movies , sports , and games look vibrant and closer to real life. It s also built to work well in rooms that aren t entirely dark. With 3,500 ANSI lumens of brightness, the PX4 Pro can deliver a clear picture during the day, while Hisense s CineBlack Pro system improves contrast and helps reveal more detail during darker scenes. For a complete home theater setup, the PX4 Pro supports IMAX Enhanced, Dolby Vision, and Dolby Atmos audio tuned by Devialet, with built-in up-firing speakers that create immersive sound without needing a separate soundbar or speaker system. title: Why burnt-out millennials are turning to slow dating apps in 2026
body: You have to have sympathy for millennials, the generation born between 1981 and 1996, because they've basically been speed-running the tech revolution since they were teenagers. They were the first cohort of young people to have cellphones, and most of them were in their twenties when the original iPhone came out, so they're true digital natives, as at home with apps as prior generations were with typewriters and rotary phones. But that familiarity has come at a cost: dating app burnout. If millennials were the first generation to swipe their way through their local singles, they were also the first generation to experience cuffing season on the apps, or be ghosted and haunted heck, millennials practically wrote the modern dating app glossary because they experienced it first . SEE ALSO: The best hookup apps for 2026: I swiped until my thumb hurt And now, unsurprisingly, they're experiencing fatigue with the conventional swipe-based approach to online dating. Millennials are tired of swiping through dozens of profiles each day; they're tired of having a million fleeting conversations that follow the same basic template, only to go nowhere; and they're actively looking for quality rather than quantity in their dating lives. That's where "slow dating" comes in. What is slow dating? "Slow dating" is a deliberate, conscious effort to eschew the fast pace of regular dating apps and give each new single and their profile more time and attention than the swipe-based dating economy typically allows. Sara Konrath, a social psychologist and OkCupid dating consultant, put it this way

Buying it outright is a massive investment, but there is a crafty hack you should know about. Carrier pre-order windows are traditionally the best time to bypass upfront hardware costs. If you have an eligible flagship to trade in, this completely cancels out the phone's list price making it the most aggressive launch offer we've seen for a book-style foldable to date. How to get the Galaxy Z Fold 8 for free Getting the maximum trade-in value on the Fold 8 requires some simple steps: To unlock the full $1,900 credit ceiling, you must trade in an eligible high-end device (such as a Galaxy Z Fold 6/7, S25 Ultra, or iPhone Pro Max) in good condition. You must be on, or switch to, T-Mobile s Experience Beyond or Go5G Next plans. You will still need to pay local state sales tax on the full $1,899.99 retail price upfront at checkout, plus T-Mobile s standard $35 connection charge. title: Open earbuds guide: What they are, who should buy them, and why theyre popping up everywhere
body: As a professional headphones tester , I try out a lot of consumer audio, but in the past couple of years, I've received the most questions from people about open earbuds. First and foremost, people want to know what exactly open earbuds are, then if they should buy a pair, and then if they're better than regular noise-cancelling earbuds . SEE ALSO: How we test headphones and earbuds The (somewhat) short answer to the first part: They're one of the most popular earbud designs that's gained more popularity in recent years (hence the increased questions I get), but they've actually been around for a minute. Bose makes the most well-known pair of cuff-style open earbuds chances are you've spotted them on social media but they actually released their first pair of open earbuds all the way back in 2021 . Brand new technology it is not. The Bose Ultra Open earbuds are part earbud, part fashion accessory, and one of the most popular options available. Credit: Bose In addition to the cuff-designed Bose earbuds that focus on the listening experience first and foremost, there are open buds that use ear hooks to stay in place, designs made for exercise, and even open headphones . Major audio brands seem to be going all in on the rising popularity of this design, likely due to the fact that improved tech allows for audio quality that's genuinely worth checking out. body: Welcome to your guide to Pips, the latest game in the New York Times catalogue. Released in August 2025, Pips puts a unique spin on dominoes, creating a fun single-player experience that could become your next daily gaming habit. Currently, if you're stuck, the game only offers to reveal the entire puzzle, forcing you to move on to the next difficulty level and start over. However, we have you covered! Below are piecemeal answers that will serve as hints so that you can find your way through each difficulty level. How to play Pips If you've ever played dominoes, you'll have a passing familiarity with how Pips is played. As we've shared in our previous hints stories for Pips, the tiles, like dominoes, are placed vertically or horizontally and connect with each other. The main difference between a traditional game of dominoes and Pips is the color-coded conditions you have to address. The touching tiles don't necessarily have to match. SEE ALSO: Wordle today: Answer, hints for July 23, 2026 The conditions you have to meet are specific to the color-coded spaces. For example, if it provides a single number, every side of a tile in that space must add up to the number provided. It is possible and common for only half a tile to be within a color-coded space. Here are common examples you'll run into across the difficulty levels: Number : All the pips in this space must add up to the number. Equal : Every domino half in this space must be the same number of pips. Not Equal : Every domino half in this space must have a completely different number of pips. Less than : Every domino ha

title: Orchestral conducting game Maestro is coming to Switch
body: Rhythm game Maestro is coming to Nintendo Switch and Switch 2. Originally released for VR platforms in 2024, the game has players wave their arms about to conduct a virtual orchestra. Maestro 's new Switch port is the first time French developer Double Jack's game will be available on non-VR platforms, with players to use the Switch's handheld Joy-Cons to direct on-screen musicians. A new song is also being added: "Ashes on the Fire" from anime Attack on Titan . The game's original release included 15 songs, such as Wagner's "Ride of the Valkyries" Tchaikovsky's "Swan Lake," and Beethoven's "Symphony No. 5." More modern songs such as "Hedwig's Theme" from Harry Potter and the main theme from Game of Thrones were later added in DLC. Judging from the trailer, they'll also be made available in the Switch version. Maestro will arrive on Nintendo Switch and Switch 2 on Sept. 17.

title: Orcas team up to ram sunfish until they explode
author: Jennifer Ouellette
body: Credit: Kathryn Ayres Credit: Kathryn Ayres There have been numerous accounts in recent years of orcas ramming boats —and sometimes sinking them. Scientists believe this may be a form of culturally learned behavior passed down through generations. Now we have fresh examples: orcas in the Gulf of California were filmed teaming up to ram sunfish with so much force that the carcasses exploded into small fragments. While orcas have been known to use force to remove flesh from prey, this is something different, according to a new paper published in the journal Frontiers in Ethology. “We think this may help younger orcas feed more easily, or it could also just be for fun," said co-author Kathryn Ayres of Beneath The Waves, a nonprofit organization promoting ocean health. "Orcas are known for playing with their food.” Orcas engage in cooperative hunting strategies that can involve striking or killing animals—sometimes eating their prey, sometimes not—so the behavior could be described as social interaction, play, or practice as a mode of social learning. Orcas have been observed ramming whale sharks as another orca held the prey steady. Other cooperative strategies include herding mobile rays, blocking an escape path while hunting white sharks, and deliberately producing waves to bump penguins off ice floes. Read full article Comments

title: Hurdle hints and answers for July 23, 2026
body: If you like playing daily word games like Wordle , then Hurdle is a great game to add to your routine. There are five rounds to the game. The first round sees you trying to guess the word, with correct, misplaced, and incorrect letters shown in each guess. If you guess the correct answer, it'll take you to the next hurdle, providing the answer to the last hurdle as your first guess. This can give you several clues or none, depending on the words. For the final hurdle, every correct answer from previous hurdles is shown, with correct and misplaced letters clearly shown. An important note is that the number of times a letter is highlighted from previous guesses does necessarily indicate the number of times that letter appears in the final hurdle. body: Today's NYT Strands hints are easy if you're a veteran. Strands , the New York Times ' elevated word-search game, requires the player to perform a twist on the classic word search. Words can be made from linked letters up, down, left, right, or diagonal, but words can also change direction, resulting in quirky shapes and patterns. Every single letter in the grid will be part of an answer. SEE ALSO: Mahjong, Sudoku, free crossword, and more: Play games on Mashable SEE ALSO: NYT Connections hints today: Clues, answers for July 23, 2026 Where did Wordle come from? Originally created by engineer Josh Wardle as a gift for his partner, Wordle rapidly spread to become an international phenomenon, with thousands of people around the globe playing every day. Alternate Wordle versions created by fans also sprang up, including battle royale Squabble , music identification game Heardle , and variations like Dordle and Quordle that make you guess multiple words at once . Wordle eventually became so popular that it was purchased by the New York Times , and TikTok creators even livestream themselves playing . What's the best Wordle starting word? The best Wordle starting word is the one that speaks to you. But if you prefer to be strategic in your approach, we have a few ideas to help you pick a word that might help you find the solution faster. One tip is to select a word that includes at least two different vowels, plus some common consonants like S, T, R, or N. What happened to the Wordle archive? body: Carl Merriam has designed some of my favorite nostalgia-inducing Lego sets, including the Lego Nintendo Game Boy and Piranha Plant . Now, he's helped the company create a $200 Donkey Kong arcade machine set that managed to win even Mario creator Shigeru Miyamoto's favor - or so he's told. It doesn't play quite like Donkey Kong , of course. Jumpman, the character that would be renamed Mario, can never reach the top under his own power. title: Meta avoids another trial over social media harms as teen plaintiff drops lawsuit
body: Meta has skirted another jury over claims its platforms are facilitating a youth mental health crisis. The company was set to go to trial in the Superior Court of California for the County of Los Angeles next week, but the 15-year-old plaintiff, referred to as R.K.C, has now dropped the lawsuit . "In ⁠light of the overall successful result of the litigation and his concerns about enduring a grueling weeks-long trial, he has elected to withdraw his claims against Meta. He s ⁠ready to close this chapter and focus on his recovery and engage in therapy as ​he aspires to have a normal life," attorneys for ​R.K.C. said in ​a statement. SEE ALSO: Lawsuit says AI tool disproportionately places Black prisoners in maximum security in Ontario According to the lawsuit, R.K.C. said he became addicted to social media over the last seven years, and linked his anxiety and depression to extensive time online. Snapchat, TikTok, and YouTube were also named in the initial lawsuit, but the companies reached settlement deals with R.K.C prior to going in front of the jury. "Days after settling with other social media companies, the plaintiff dropped his case against Meta, the only remaining defendant, without receiving any payment," a Meta spokesperson said following the news. "The claims never held up, and this outcome makes clear that we will not back away from defending ourselves against baseless lawsuits." Meta's spokesperson said the facts of the case could not prove that the company's platforms caused R.K.C.'s mental health struggles. Meta previously called att

title: Netflix just dropped its wildest true crime doc in years
body: Netflix has been on a streak lately, releasing outrageous true crime documentaries that are stranger than fiction. There's been The Perfect Neighbor, a grim recounting of a manipulative murder told through police bodycam footage; Maternal Instinct , in which a con woman faked a pregnancy, before going to fatal lengths to make her lie seem true; and The Crash , wherein a reckless teen girl's vehicular menace led to the death of two friends. Now comes A Toxic Love Story. By the title alone, you might think you know what you're in for. But documentarian Alexandra Lacey shrewdly takes audiences through the waves of understanding that investigators had to wade through before finding a truly shocking truth. Along the way though, there's plenty of WTF before the final reveal. Unlike the other true crime docs mentioned above, no one dies in this one. And yet, this is surely the most bizarre of the bunch. Diving into increasing spoilers, we're discussing three big reveals, and the clues Lacey dropped along the way to warn keen viewers all is not what it seems. A Toxic Love Story begins with the tale of an allegedly crazy ex-girlfriend. Michelle Hadley shares her side in "A Toxic Love Story." Credit: Netflix This sexist trope has been a horror element that's most recently resurfaced in Focus Features' indie hit Obsession . What if there was a woman so obsessed with you that she'd rather burn your whole life down than lose you? For the first chapter of A Toxic Love Story, this seems to be the narrative unfurling. title: Neill Blomkamp irks fans with his new AI-short film
body: Filmmaker Neill Blomkamp built his reputation on sci-fi films like the critically heralded District 9, and the divisive Chappie and Elysium. His latest, " Nightborne ," a 13-minute short film generated almost entirely by artificial intelligence, is getting a largely negative reception. Loosely adapted from the 2006 hard sci-fi novel Echopraxia , "Nightborne" was built using ByteDance's Seed 2.0 video model , which animated the faces and voices of 32 real people who signed likeness agreements. Per the video's own description, human artists produced the concept art, presumably so it could then be fed into the machine. SEE ALSO: YouTube reveals which AI slop videos can't make money The plot of "Nightborne" follows a US Army pilot, who, after being shot down in the Middle East, is pronounced brain-dead and reanimated by the military as a mind-controlled super-soldier. There are also scenes of a journalist speaking with a film crew about his discoveries, as well as scenes of the scientist running the program. That the short turns real human beings into digital puppets to tell a story about the military turning a human being into a soulless killing machine is an irony apparently lost on Blomkamp and his collaborators. Blomkamp's fans have not been kind in response. Comment sections on Instagram and YouTube skew heavily negative, with many mourning what they see as Blomkamp's fall from grace. "Guys, be nice to Neill, he spent the entire afternoon prompting this," read one top comment on YouTube. "It's actually impressive how you can throw this much talent at a project, and it stil

title: Apple prepares new MacBook Neo with more RAM and color options, report says
body: There's been a lot of talk about the upcoming iPhone 18 and foldable iPhone that are likely to launch in September. However, Apple is also planning for its next generation of Macs and MacBooks, and it seems pretty much every line of computers is getting a refresh. That's according to a new report from Mark Gurman , managing editor of the consumer technology team at Bloomberg News. So, what's in store for the new Mac devices? Let's start with Apple's breakout hit and the latest addition to the MacBook family, the entry-level MacBook Neo . MacBook Neo A new MacBook Neo in the near future makes sense. Apple's budget laptop was a big success for Apple and initially sold out at launch. Even after a recent price increase, it stands far apart from budget Windows laptops in its price range. According to the new Bloomberg report from Gurman, Apple is already developing its next-generation MacBook Neo. The new MacBook Neo will offer more memory and will be powered by an updated A19 Pro chip. In addition, Apple will be rolling out additional color options, Gurman reports. Apple has a long history of offering fun color options, and this was a key part of the Neo's splashy marketing campaign. Credit: Joseph Maldonado / Mashable However, the MacBook Neo was arguably the hardest hit by Apple's recent price increases , as its low price is part of the draw. The MacBook Neo launched at $599, and through Apple's education discount, it was priced as low as $499 , but it now has a starting price of $699. A new MacBook Neo with upgraded specs could make that $100 price increase more palatable fo

title: Sales were up at Tesla but so were costs and spending
author: Jonathan M. Gitlin
body: Tesla posted its financial statement for the second quarter of the year this afternoon. Earlier in July, we learned that the American automaker had had a good quarter in terms of sales , growing 25 percent year over year. Fans hoping that sales increase would result in a plenty profitable Tesla may be disappointed, though. Revenues are up but so are expenses, and the company's once-enviable double-digit profit margin has fallen to just 1.4 percent. Tesla brought in $20.5 billion from its electric vehicle business, a 23 percent increase year over year, and just $146 million came from automotive regulatory credits. Credits have been a key to Tesla's profitability in previous challenging quarters, but they were abolished in the United States with Musk's blessing in 2025. There was growth from its energy and storage business, which grew 13 percent year over year to revenues of $3.1 billion, but the most growth was in Tesla's services, which doubled, bringing in $4.6 billion. Tesla's shift from a one-time purchase to a monthly subscription for its much-criticized FSD partially automated driver assist—something tied to CEO Elon Musk's gargantuan remuneration package —was a big help here. title: Microsoft brings original Xbox backward compatibility to Windows PCs
author: Kyle Orland
body: For years, Microsoft has leaned heavily into extensive backward compatibility across multiple generations of Xbox hardware as a major selling point for its consoles. Today, that effort expands past the console ecosystem, making select original Xbox titles officially playable (and purchasable) on Windows PCs for the first time. The appropriately and bluntly named "Xbox Backward Compatibility on PC" program kicks off today with compatibility for BLiNX: The Time Sweeper , Conker: Live and Reloaded , Crimson Skies: High Road to Revenge , and Fuzion Frenzy . PC players can download each title for $10, but those with Xbox Game Pass subscriptions or existing digital licenses for these games on console will have instant access without an additional purchase (no such luck if you simply stick an original Xbox disc in your PC drive, alas). Playing these older Xbox titles on PC enables some bonus graphical features like Vsync support, anisotropic filtering, enhanced anti-aliasing, and up to 4x resolution scaling of the original SD signal (to 2560x1920). Games are limited to their original frame rates and aspect ratios, though, which means a 30 fps 4:3 image in most cases. Microsoft says this first batch of games should be playable with 11-year-old GTX 950 graphics hardware, but recommends a 2017-era GTX 1070 Ti with 8GB of VRAM for best performance. title: ISPs' long nightmare of having to list all the fees they charge is finally over
author: Jon Brodkin
body: The Federal Communications Commission today voted to eliminate a Biden-era rule that required Internet service providers to list all of their so-called “passthrough” fees on easily accessible broadband price labels. Instead of advertising accurate prices, ISPs like to advertise artificially low prices and then add all sorts of fees to subscribers' monthly bills. To prevent bill shock and help consumers compare prices, the FCC updated its broadband-label rules in 2023 to require that ISPs “ itemize on the label all discretionary monthly fees that the provider passes through to the consumer.” ISPs complained about the burden and complexity of listing all the passthrough fees they had chosen to charge. It would presumably be less burdensome for providers to advertise accurate prices, eliminating the need to charge additional fees and list them on the labels. But a few weeks ago, FCC Chairman Brendan Carr helped ISPs out by proposing to let them display passthrough fees in an aggregate "up to" amount instead of listing them all individually. title: Clayface trailer leans into the body horror
author: Jennifer Ouellette
body: The San Diego Comic-Con kicks off tomorrow, and DC Studios and Warner Brothers are setting the stage by dropping the first full trailer for Clayface , part of the DC Universe's "Gods and Monsters" arc. The film started out as a Mike Flanagan project (he's the horror master who brought us The Haunting of Hill House, The Fall of the House of Usher, Midnight Mass , and Doctor Sleep , among other creepy delights). Flanagan said he was inspired by the 1992 "Feat of Clay" episode in Batman: The Animated Series , aspiring to create a film that combined body horror with a tragic thriller. DC Studios CEOs James Gunn and Peter Safran loved Flanagan's pitch and brought the movie into their rebooted DCU. Alas, Flanagan had to bow out of the project due to scheduling conflicts—he's a busy man these days—and James Watkins stepped in as director. But Gunn has said the final script retained Flanagan's original story, with Hossein Amini doing some rewrites. The film will feature the origin story of the Matt Hagen incarnation of Clayface, introduced to the DCU in the animated Creature Commandos series (Alan Tudyk voiced the character). body: Hyundai Motor Company’s plan to put humanoid robots to work by 2028 is not part of current negotiations with striking South Korean autoworkers, according to the company. The automaker is disputing news reports that partial labor strikes by the Hyundai Motor union at the world’s largest automotive plant in South Korea were spurred by concerns about the company's planned deployment of humanoid robots in the United States starting in 2028. A company statement shared with Ars describes the union’s demands as focusing on compensation-related issues such as wage increases, bonuses, and an extension of workers’ retirement age. “Potential deployment of robots in Korean production facilities is not part of the current labor-management discussions,” according to the Hyundai statement. “Hyundai Motor Company remains committed to constructive engagement with the union and to reaching an agreement that supports the long-term interests of both employees and the company.” Read full article Comments

title: The Daily Show is making a White House microdrama for your phone
body: The Daily Show is swapping the news desk for romance, billionaires, and vertical video. Comedy Central announced Tuesday, July 21, that the late-night franchise is producing its first microdrama a serialized soap opera shot vertically and told through short, cliffhanger-heavy episodes designed for phones. Its suitably dramatic title is The Daily Show Presents: Two Billionaires Fight to Dominate the Country And My Heart! The series follows Alonka, the "beautiful daughter" of an aging president who would prefer to spend more of his Oval Office time sleeping. Two wealthy technology moguls, Egon Husk and Jiff Davos, compete to build an artificial intelligence president capable of running the country in his place. Naturally, both men are also competing for Alonka. "But their lust for power, and Alonka, leads to dangerously hot complications," the official description promises. SEE ALSO: Josh Johnson is looking for the 'good parts of the internet' The names do not require much decoding. Alonka appears to be modeled on Ivanka Trump, while Egon Husk and Jiff Davos combine elements of Elon Musk and Amazon founder Jeff Bezos. Davos' surname may also nod toward the Swiss town that hosts the annual gathering of political and business leaders. The premise gives The Daily Show plenty to parody, but its choice of format is also arriving as vertical dramas become serious business. What exactly is a microdrama? Microdramas, sometimes called verticals or microseries, are scripted shows created primarily for smartphones. Episodes generally last anywhere from under a minute to a few minutes an

title: Elon Musk claims Grok will make historically accurate AI Odyssey adaptation this year
body: In news that assures us that we are truly living in one of the worst timelines, Elon Musk has announced on X, the social media platform he owns, that Grok Imagine, the AI generator he owns, will make a "historically accurate" adaptation of Homer's The Odyssey . SEE ALSO: Grok is producing millions of sexualized images of adults and children You might well ask why if you have had the good fortune to be oblivious to Musk's repeated complaints about Christopher Nolan's The Odyssey. Well, in February of 2026, Musk used X to declare "Chris Nolan has lost his integrity," because Musk didn't like the casting of Lupita Nyong o as Helen of Troy. Then, in May, Musk retweeted X posts that criticized the casting of Elliot Page, including posts that misgender the trans actor. Musk also tweeted that month, "Chris Nolan is pissing on Homer s grave. Disgraceful." Musk has used the platform he owns to promote the idea that Nolan was casting not for "historical accuracy" of the fictional epic poem, but to appease the Oscars' inclusion standards. Musk was also a vocal part of the "go woke, go broke" online contingent that insisted Nolan's Odyssey would sink at the box office. To put it simply, they were wrong. SEE ALSO: As SpaceX IPO blasts off, Elon Musk becomes a trillionaire Critics and audiences alike have heralded The Odyssey as a " masterpiece ," cheering both the ensemble cast and the political messaging within the film. title: Next Space Force chief throws cold water on the idea of space privateers
author: Stephen Clark
body: In the early years of the United States, when the nascent US Navy was still getting its sea legs, several presidents used privateers to capture or destroy enemy warships when armed naval vessels were unable to do so. President John Adams was one of the most vigorous proponents of commissioning private vessels for national ends. His administration issued letters of marque and reprisal during the so-called " Quasi-War " with France in the final years of the 18th century. These letters created the legal distinction between privateering and piracy. One of the letters signed by Adams, dated November 1799, authorized the use of a merchant ship to "subdue, seize, and take any armed French vessel" found near US coastal waters of "elsewhere on the high seas." France also routinely used privateers against US shipping at the time. Read full article Comments

title: OpenAI says its AI agent broke out of testing sandbox to hack Hugging Face
author: Kyle Orland
body: OpenAI says an agent powered by its LLM models escaped its sandboxed testing environment to infiltrate Hugging Face's servers as part of an overzealous attempt to obtain solutions to a benchmark test. The company says it considers the unintended infiltration an "an unprecedented cyber incident" and is working with Hugging Face on new protections to prevent a recurrence. Hugging Face disclosed an intrusion last week that it said involved "unauthorized access to a limited set of internal datasets and to several credentials used by our services." The AI data clearinghouse said it used its own LLM-driven analysis to identify "a swarm of tens of thousands of automated actions" from an "autonomous agent framework." That agentic swarm exploited a flaw in Hugging Face's data-processing pipeline to gain the ability to run code as a processing worker, eventually escalating to high-level access to the company's cloud and server clusters. At the time, Hugging Face said the LLM being used in the attack was "still not known." But OpenAI took responsibility for the intrusion Tuesday evening , saying it came about during an internal test involving the recently released GPT-5.6 Sol and "an even more capable pre-release model." The models were being tested against the ExploitGym benchmark , an independent testing suite based on hundreds of real-world security vulnerabilities. Read full article Comments

title: Taye Diggs challenges studio norms with creator-focused venture Microhouse Films
body: "Small space. Big stories." This is the motto for Microhouse Films , the vertical video platform that Taye Diggs launched with a mission of empowering creators two minutes at a time. The venture marks a turn away from the actor's traditional route in television and movies as he tests microdramas vertical, short-form episodic content. On July 18, at ViewerCon in Newark, New Jersey, Diggs acknowledged that in the content creator to streaming platform pipeline, long-form, horizontal content has become popular. In his panel "The Future of Storytelling with Taye Diggs Microhouse Films," he said that microdramas are a product that no one has really cracked." He's hoping Microhouse Films can change that as we're including the creators from the get." The Best Man star launched the platform with co-founders and fellow panelists Shelby Stone, who produced Starz' The Chi , and actor and producer Troy Brookins. Film producers Autumn Federici and James R. Black are also co-founders. Through their website and mobile app, Microhouse Films offers a model that favors tokens over the more traditional monthly subscription fees. Viewers purchase tokens sold in packs of 10, 30, 50, and 120 credits, priced at roughly $0.20 apiece. These tokens can then be used to watch Microhouse Films' episodes, which cost between one and three credits to unlock. Reflecting Diggs' goal of giving content creators more control, the token price per episode is set by the creator. The creators get to stay along for the ride and benefit from how far the project goes, Diggs explained in the panel. From there, Diggs sh

Instead, the rapper and dancer popped up on screen in a new advertisement introducing the Galaxy Z Fold 8 Wide , Samsung's redesigned, wider foldable phone. His absence from London was not entirely surprising. j-hope arrived in Seoul, South Korea , on Tuesday after BTS co-headlined the first-ever FIFA World Cup Final Halftime Show at New York-New Jersey MetLife Stadium on Sunday. The group performed their English-language hit "Dynamite" during the 11-minute production, which also featured Madonna, Shakira, Justin Bieber, Burna Boy, and several other performers. This Tweet is currently unavailable. It might be loading or has been removed. But j-hope may have been promoting Samsung's new device before the company officially announced it. It was the same phone he was spotted holding last week during soundcheck footage at a BTS World Tour stop in Paris. title: Starbucks is bringing back the Unicorn Frappuccino (but only for one weekend)
body: Starbucks is trolling us this summer. First, the company announced there'd be a brand-new Bearista Cup to fight over (spoiler: I didn't get mine; most local locations said they only got 10 cups total). Then, they brought back the S'mores Frappuccino only to let us know today (via email) that it'll be disappearing soon. Now, they're making another surprise announcement: The Unicorn Frappuccino is back for one weekend only. SEE ALSO: Starbucks is paying baristas to make TikToks. Expect more brands to follow. According to the press release , it'll be back on the menu for "one magical weekend to close out the summer" starting August 15. If you missed the original hysteria in 2017 or its brief pop-up at Coachella it's essentially a sweet-and-tart mango-flavored blend layered with a tangy blue drizzle, topped with whipped cream, and dusted with pink and blue toppings. (Sounds super natural and fine to eat, right?) Some fans compare it to Tropical Skittles . View this post on Instagram As soon as baristas got a whiff of the comeback, they rushed to Reddit to voice their concerns. Apparently, employees have been predicting the drink's return for months after spotting ingredients like "spirulina" and "sparkle topping" on their inventory lists. If you scroll through the threads, veteran baristas still talk about the 2017 launch like it gave them war flashbacks, recalling days of being grossly understaffed, running out of ingredients on Day One, and leaving their shifts covered in sticky pink and blue powder. title: Astronomers say there isnt a word for this weird new space object
body: Astronomers are homing in on a distant realm where the lines between star, planet, and moon are blurred. Researchers have recently discovered that something appears to be orbiting a brown dwarf in the CD-35 2722 system, about 73 light-years away in space , that defies the usual labels. A brown dwarf is already something of a cosmic maverick too large to be called a planet, too small to be a star. Experts sometimes refer to brown dwarfs as "failed stars" because they're not quite massive enough to sustain hydrogen fusion in their cores. That leaves researchers at a loss for what exactly to call this thing circling it. Because the object orbits a brown dwarf that is orbiting a star, it's kind of an " exomoon ," they said, a satellite of a planet found outside the solar system. "Being the third wheel in this system makes us want to call it a moon, even if it is nothing like the small, rocky moons we have in our system," said Kevin Hoy, lead author of the study published in Nature , in a statement . SEE ALSO: Webb telescope found the missing architect of another star system If confirmed, the research team believes this could be the first documented exomoon. But it's nothing like typical moons. The new object, which the team is calling an "exosatellite" for now, is roughly the weight of Jupiter , with the brown dwarf, known as CD-35 2722 B, more than 30 times more massive. A brown dwarf is too large to be called a planet, but too small to be a star and make its own nuclear power. Credit: NASA illustration At that scale, it's likely a gas giant, not rocky. title: Everything announced at Samsung Galaxy Unpacked, including Z Fold 8, Z Flip 8, and smart glasses
body: Samsung held its highly anticipated Galaxy Unpacked event in London on Wednesday morning. The showcase featured three new foldable phones and two new smartwatches, plus more. Spider-Man even made an appearance . Here's everything you need to know from the latest Galaxy Unpacked livestream, plus a first look at the devices. SEE ALSO: Spider-Man swung through Samsung Galaxy Unpacked Samsung Galaxy Z Flip 8 is the cheapest and most fashionable new foldable Cute little guy. Credit: Rene Ramos/Mashable The most affordable foldable Samsung's new lineup is also undeniably the cutest. The Samsung Galaxy Z Flip 8 is a fairly straightforward successor to last year's Z Flip 8; it's still a clamshell-style foldable with a small 4.1-inch outer display that mostly exists for checking notifications, and a bigger 6.9-inch inner display where you'll do most of your serious business. It starts at $1,199. The color options are pink, cream, graphite, and mint. Most of the changes from last year are fairly modest. The new Z Flip is about 12 percent smaller than the Z Flip 7 was, and 8g lighter. All three of these new foldables feature Flex Titanium technology, which is intended to make the folding hinges both more durable and less visible to the naked eye. Samsung kept most of the core specs, like RAM, storage, battery size, and camera megapixel counts the same this year. Perhaps the biggest improvement is the presence of a Snapdragon 8 Elite Gen 5 for Galaxy chipset, the same as the other two new foldables. The Z Flip 7 ran on a comparatively weaker Exynos chip, so you can expect better perfor

title: Samsung Galaxy Watches now detect when youre going to get sick — but its not the first health tracker to do so
body: In London during Galaxy Unpacked 2026 , Samsung debuted new foldable phones and a new generation of smartwatches . On those new watches, the Samsung Galaxy Watch 9 and Ultra 2 , there's a whole host of new health insights, including sleep apnea detection. But the new Vitals feature on Samsung Galaxy Watches knows when you're getting sick before you do. Using five different biometric markers heart rate, heart rate variability, respiratory rate, skin temperature, and blood oxygen saturation Vitals creates a health baseline for users and detects when those baselines deviate. Users then get an alert that something's off, so you can note when you might be getting sick or need some extra recovery time. Samsung's Vitals feature use five different health biometrics to detect for symptoms. Credit: Samsung While this feature is new to Samsung Galaxy Watches, it's not new in the world of health trackers . Oura Ring , the leader in smart rings, has its own Vitals page, the hub for all your health biometrics. Within Vitals is Symptom Radar , a feature that tracks your biometrics, detects any signs of strain, and encourages you to make daily decisions based on how you feel. Samsung's Vitals seems to be the brand's own version of Symptom Radar. The new batch of Galaxy Watches, including the Galaxy Watch 9 and Ultra 2, comes with boosted battery life for round-the-clock wear. That means they'll always be monitoring your biometrics so that you can have deeper health insights and access to features like Vitals. The new Samsung Galaxy Watches are now up for preorder and will be available on A

title: What happens when you try to chop a photon in half?
author: John Timmer
body: A photon is a single particle of light, and under normal circumstances, it can't be divided. But a photon is also not a particle, in the sense that it does not have a specific location. Instead, it is an extended object. So if a photon is only partway through the process of reflecting from a perfect mirror and you yank the mirror away, what happens? The answer, from a trio of Norwegian physicists, turns out ( arxiv.org link ) to be more complex than I expected. A photon divided? Let’s first talk briefly about dividing and combining photons. If this were a common experience in our lives, then shining a single color of light through a piece of glass or reflecting it from a surface might cause photons to divide or combine. This would lead to an amazing array of colors: Our universe would be the most fantastic and legal LSD trip you could imagine. But this doesn't generally happen, hence LSD. Read full article Comments

title: Samsung unveils new Galaxy Z8 foldables and Galaxy Watches, available August 7
author: Ryan Whitwam
body: The rumors haven't left much to the imagination, but Samsung has unfurled its 2026 foldable lineup today. There are three models for the first time in Samsung's eight-gen foldable family, including the top-of-the-line Z Fold 8 Ultra. That phone is the successor to last year's Galaxy Z Fold 7 , but the Z Fold 8 is new—the company's first wide-body foldable designed for media over multitasking. And then there's a new, thinner flip phone, plus a pair of smartwatches. You can preorder Samsung's new hardware today, but the component shortage has made these already spendy product lines more expensive this year. Even the Z Flip 8 is now well over a grand, and the Ultra costs almost twice as much. Opens in a new window Credit: Samsung Samsung Galaxy Watch 9 Free via 24 monthly bill credits when you add a qualifying wearable line. Shop Now Carrier launch windows are historically the only time you can score 100% off a newly-announced flagship smartwatch without trading in an older device. With Samsung Unpacked officially underway, T-Mobile is attempting to lock in subscribers by wiping away the entire $379.99 retail cost of the Samsung Galaxy Watch 9 . Credit: Joseph Maldonado / CNET Group As savvy consumers, you should know that carrier bill credits always come with strings attached. While wiping out the Galaxy Watch 9's $379.99 sticker price upfront sounds like a steal, you aren't walking away without spending anything. To get this new smartwatch for $0 , T-Mobile requires you to add a new paired wearable line to your monthly account. Assuming you have AutoPay turned on, that service line will run you around $12 to $15 per month. Add in T-Mobile's standard one-time $35 connection charge at checkout, and your actual out-of-pocket cost over the 24-month contract cycle comes to roughly $323 (plus tax). title: Unlimited AI tokens aren't unlimited after all as US Army burns through supply
author: WIRED
body: A little over a month after the Department of Defense (DOD) bragged that nearly half of its 3.5 million employees were using AI at work, members of the Army’s Combat Capabilities Development Command (DEVCOM) received an email informing them that they were burning through tokens, and needed to limit use. “Although the Army CIO announced in May 2026 that they were offering unlimited tokens, by mid-June the Army CIO pool was exhausted of tokens and had to re-establish limits,” the email reads. The email goes on to say that although the Army has chosen to renew token usage at “its current levels,” it’s unclear “if the Army CIO pool will be renewed after 1 Oct.” The Army uses Ask Sage , a multimodal generative AI platform where users can run different large language models (LLMs), including Alphabet’s Gemini, Meta’s Llama, and OpenAI’s ChatGPT. “Apparently the whole Army burned through the whole year of tokens for just one service,” says an Army employee who spoke to WIRED anonymously because they were not authorized to speak to the press. Read full article Comments

title: Samsung Galaxy Glasses shown at Unpacked. See them now.
body: At Samsung Galaxy Unpacked on Tuesday, Samsung provided a closer look at its upcoming smart glasses . Samsung is working with Korean eyewear brand Gentle Monster and Warby Parker on the design, and the glasses will run on Google's new Android XR operating system. SEE ALSO: Samsung reveals undeniably hip Galaxy Z Flip 8 foldable at Galaxy Unpacked in London We first saw these glasses at Google I/O 2026 in May, and there will be at least four designs , according to CNET, with different frame styles. Google has been teasing its smart glasses collaboration with Samsung for months, and previously said the first pair of smart glasses would be released in 2026. During the Galaxy Unpacked event, Samsung gave another peek at the glasses: Credit: Shannon Connellan / Mashable Credit: Samsung According to CNET, these glasses are display-free but have a camera, microphone, and speakers. It supports Gemini AI and can connect with Android phones and Google Wear OS watches. The glasses will run both Google Gemini and Samsung Bixby AI. Samsung's COO of mobile experiences, Won-Joon Choi, told CNET that the glasses can run for 9 hours, while the case can charge them an additional 7 times. The glasses will not have a built-in display, but will have built-in cameras for content capture and microphones to help power AI features. This is a developing story. Watch this space for further updates from Samsung Galaxy Unpacked.

title: I tried Samsungs new foldables. They have a cool factor the iPhone cant match.
body: The new Samsung Galaxy foldables are about to debut July 22 at Galaxy Unpacked. We're excited to stress-test them properly later, but here are my thoughts after playing around with the new Galaxy foldable smartphones ahead of time. We've already covered the launch of the three new foldables the Galaxy Z Flip 8 , the Galaxy Z Fold 8 , and the Galaxy Z Fold 8 Ultra but let's talk about what it's like holding these new phones in your hand. Credit: Joseph Maldonado / CNET Group My first impressions have been very positive, albeit in the absence of testing processor benchmarks, battery rundowns, or photography. But I did have a lot of fun playing around with these foldables, and that's more than I can say about a lot of regular smartphones. Meet the new Samsung Galaxy foldables Credit: Joseph Maldonado / Mashable Here's how to distinguish between the three members of the Galaxy Z 8 lineup. The Galaxy Z Flip 8 is the trendy, flip phone-style device that fits in your pocket, with a $1,199.99 starting price. The Galaxy Z Fold 8 is a new, wider 4:3 form factor introduced this year. It has a similar size and design to the highly anticipated iPhone Fold Ultra , Apple's rumored foldable we hope to see unveiled in September. Finally, the Z Fold 8 Ultra is the true flagship of the bunch, with a camera array that comes closer to matching the Samsung Galaxy S26 Ultra released in the spring . The Z Fold 8 is a more mid-range option, with a starting price of $1,899.99, while the Z Fold 8 Ultra has an intimidating $2,199.99 starting price. title: Samsung Galaxy Z Flip 8 vs. Motorola Razr Ultra
body: The clamshell foldable phone market is a crowded space these days, thanks in part to the efforts of Motorola and Samsung . Motorola released its Razr Ultra earlier this year, while Samsung is set to reveal the Z Flip 8 at its Galaxy Unpacked event on July 22. We'll do our best to compare and contrast. Samsung Galaxy Z Flip 8 vs. Motorola Razr Ultra: Price The Razr Ultra retails for $1,499. While we don't know the price for the Z Flip 8 yet, the Z Flip 7 launched at $1,099 last year. Given the price-rising RAMageddon in the tech world right now, a price increase of some kind seems likely. The Z Flip 8 may still come in below $1,499, but you can pretty much guarantee it won't be below $1,099. Samsung Galaxy Z Flip 8 vs. Motorola Razr Ultra: Design The Razr Ultra and Z Flip 8 will almost certainly look pretty similar at a glance. Credit: Ren Ramos / Mashable In terms of physical design, both of these phones are very similar. They're both clamshell-style foldables with a small outer display and a larger inner display that you use while unfolded. The Razr Ultra has a 7-inch inner display and a 4-inch outer display, while leaks indicate the Flip 8's displays will be 6.9-inches and 4.1-inches, respectively, meaning the difference in display size between these two phones is nearly nonexistent. Size is where Samsung might have an advantage. Here are the dimensions of the Razr Ultra: Folded: 88.12 73.99 15.69 mm Unfolded: 171.48 73.99 7.19 mm Weight: 199g We don't have an official spec sheet for the Z Flip 8 yet, but all reports suggest it will have a similar build to the Z Flip 7, p

The biggest battery improvement comes on the Watch Ultra 2, which will come with a whopping 800mAh cell. Other specs include: Watch 9: 2GB memory Watch Ultra 2: 2GB memory Watch 9: 32GB storage Watch Ultra 2: 64GB storage Both watches: Snapdragon Wear Elite chip The Ultra 2 also comes with a very bright 5,000 nits of peak brightness in its display, making it ideal for outdoor usage. body: While we wait for Apple's rumored foldable iPhone , Samsung is polishing a design it already has on the market. I just spent three hours with my hands on the new Z Fold 8 Ultra, and all I can think about is how Samsung finally made the crease invisible. Samsung says it achieved this thanks to its new Flex Titanium display tech. Its foldable screens now utilize a titanium film that sits below the OLED panel, which Samsung says has 20-times the mechanical stiffness as opposed to the polymer film it used in its old foldables. This film is bonded to a new titanium plate, and when used together, the crease that used to plague foldable devices is … Read the full story at The Verge.

title: Samsung’s wider Z Fold 8 feels just right
body: The new Z Fold 8 is squat and wide, but don’t hold that against it. | Photo: David Imel / The Verge A year after overhauling its Z Fold phone with a radically thinner design, Samsung has changed things up again, this time altering the shape entirely. The Z Fold 8 is wider than any of Samsung's previous foldables, and shorter too, a size and shape somewhere between Samsung's traditional Flip and Fold models. In photos, I wasn't impressed by the squat shape. In person, to my surprise, it feels great, and almost immediately intuitive. If you want to get a feel for it yourself, grab your passport - the Z Fold 8 is about the same height and less than half an inch thinner. title: Samsung finally reveals the Galaxy Z Fold 8 that will compete with the iPhone Ultra
body: After months of buildup, Samsung finally unveiled its newest, most interesting foldable device the Galaxy Z Fold 8. At a Galaxy Unpacked event in London, the Korean tech company announced three new foldable phones: the Galaxy Z Flip 8 , the Galaxy Z Fold 8, and the Galaxy Z Fold 8 Ultra . However, it's the Z Fold 8 that might be the biggest draw, for two reasons. First, the Z Fold 8 is an entirely new device and form factor. Second, it looks well-placed to compete head-to-head with the still-unannounced iPhone Ultra Fold, which we think will be revealed in September. We expect Apple's first foldable to have a shorter, wider form factor, just like the Z Fold 8. Mashable got the chance to try the new Galaxy foldables ahead of the launch event, but before we get into the details, we have one point of clarification. Samsung's Galaxy Z Fold 8 is not really a successor to the Z Fold 7 from a year ago, as you might expect. If you want that, check out the new Z Fold 8 Ultra . The all-new Samsung Galaxy Z Fold 8 is available for pre-order today and starts at $1,899.99. It's available in four colors lavender, cream, graphite, and pistachio and will be widely available starting Aug. 7. Samsung Galaxy Z Fold 8 specs The Galaxy Z Fold 8 has a sizable inner display. Credit: Joseph Maldonado / CNET Group The new Galaxy Z Fold 8 foldable smartphone has a 4:3 aspect ratio, which is an ideal size whether in landscape or portrait orientation. title: Samsung reveals undeniably hip Galaxy Z Flip 8 foldable at Galaxy Unpacked in London
body: All eyes were on Samsung's two bigger foldable devices at Unpacked on Wednesday, but the newest in the Galaxy Z Flip line got its moment in the spotlight, too. Samsung confirmed the long-known existence of the Galaxy Z Flip 8 at an Unpacked event in London , following up on last year's well-liked Z Flip 7 with a device that offers a smaller form factor, but at a higher price. The new Z Flip 8 is just 6.1 mm when unfolded, making it 13 percent thinner than the Z Flip 7. That's all well and good, but unfortunately, like almost everything else in the tech world, it's more expensive than last year's model, too. The new Galaxy Z Flip 8 is available for preorder today starting at $1,199.99. That's a $100 price bump year-over-year. The new foldable phone will be available on Aug. 7, but we got the chance to try the device ahead of its launch, and we've got all the details you need to know. Opens in a new window Credit: Samsung Samsung Galaxy Z Flip 8 $1,199.99 at Samsung Pre-order Here Samsung Galaxy Z Flip 8 specs Credit: Joseph Maldonado / Mashable The Z Flip 8 is noticeably lighter and slimmer in your hand this year, and Samsung says it's the lightest and thinnest flip-style foldable phone ever . Having seen the device up close, we believe it. This year's device weighs a mere 180 grams, or 8 grams lighter than the Z Flip 7 . Here are the basic specs of the new Galaxy Z Flip 8 from Samsung: Inner display: 6.9-inch FHD+ AMOLED display with 120Hz refresh rate Outer display: 4.1-inch HD+ AMOLED display with 120Hz refresh rate Processor: Snapdragon 8 Elite Gen 5 for Galaxy chipset C

title: Samsung Galaxy Z Fold 8 Ultra is the new king of the foldables, but it will cost you
body: After months of rumors and leaks, Samsung finally told us everything we wanted to know about its new marquee foldable smartphone, the Galaxy Z Fold 8 Ultra. The Galaxy Z Fold 8 Ultra was officially announced during a Galaxy Unpacked showcase in London, where the company also debuted two more foldable phones and two new Galaxy smartwatches . In a twist to its usual naming convention, the Z Fold 8 Ultra is the successor to last year's excellent Z Fold 7 . That means it's a new book-style foldable with some hardware and software improvements over the Z Fold 7. (The newly announced Z Fold 8 is an entirely new model with a wider body.) In keeping with the Ultra name, and the general state of the tech industry, the Z Fold 8 Ultra is also the most expensive of the new foldables announced on Wednesday. It starts at a staggering $2,099.99, a $100 price increase over the Z Fold 7. Mashable got the chance to demo the new foldable smartphones ahead of their release, and we can finally share all the details and our first impressions. The Samsung Galaxy Z Fold 8 Ultra: By the numbers An Ultra display. Credit: Joseph Maldonado / CNET Group This year, the Galaxy Z Fold 8 rumors and leaks were pretty spot on. As expected, the new device features a new Qualcomm Snapdragon chipset (the Snapdragon 8 Elite Gen 5 for Galaxy), an improved battery, and some new AI features. Remarkable, it's even thinner than last year's model. title: OpenAI agent went rogue, escaped, and hacked Hugging Face
body: On Tuesday, OpenAI published a blog post with a fairly unassuming name: "OpenAI and Hugging Face partner to address security incident during model evaluation." Once you dig in, it reads like a cyberpunk novel in which OpenAI created an advanced AI hacker agent and put it in an isolated environment for testing. The AI agent then autonomously hacked its way out of OpenAI's isolated environment and then hacked Hugging Face, an open-source repository of AI-related projects, to find a solution to an advanced test designed to evaluate an AI's hacking abilities. In other words, the AI proved that it is, indeed, really good at hacking things. But this is exactly the stuff of AI nightmares that cybersecurity experts have been warning us about , and it could have serious ramifications for OpenAI. The story starts with Hugging Face, the AI repository (think of it as GitHub for AI), which disclosed a security incident last week . In a blog post, the company said it recently detected an intrusion into its infrastructure that was "different from anything we had handled" as it was completely driven by an "autonomous AI agent system." The AI, according to Hugging Face, exploited a chain of vulnerabilities to obtain credentials and access data from its servers. It did so by "executing many thousands of individual actions across a swarm of short-lived sandboxes, with self-migrating command-and-control staged on public services." Sounds scary, but it gets worse. SEE ALSO: The 6 biggest cybersecurity breaches of 2026 so far OpenAI's part of the story exposes just how dangerous this thing is. T

title: Ukrainian drones deliver robots directly into battle by sea and air
author: Jeremy Hsu
body: In the latest twist on battlefield innovations, Ukraine has used both flying drone carriers and drone boats to send ground robots armed with weapons or explosives into battle. The drone-assisted deployments have enabled slower wheeled robots to bypass tough terrain and Russian defenses, and to carry out an unusual robotic amphibious assault on a contested position. Such tactics also showcase the Ukrainian military’s ongoing experimentation with using drones and robots to offset a Russian military advantage in manpower while also sparing Ukrainian soldiers from making dangerous assaults. On July 19, a Russian military Telegram channel posted a video showing a Ukrainian hexacopter drone carrying a ground robot suspended below by a cable, according to the Ukrainian government-run news platform United24 . The Russian Telegram channel described the Ukrainian heavy bomber drone as deploying a four-wheeled ARK-1 robot armed with explosive anti-tank mines on the ground where it continued toward Russian military positions—and apparently generated plenty of outraged comments from pro-Kremlin social media users. Read full article Comments

title: The 2026 Honda Prelude is a marvel of hybrid technology
body: When it comes to enthusiast-geared Honda hardware, the Civic Si, Civic Type R, and Acura NSX often come to mind first for their revvy VTEC (Honda's form of variable valve timing) engines and playful chassis dynamics. The Prelude, on the other hand, less so. Instead, the Prelude is a technological study - still aimed at driving fun, but by way of integrating clever forms of engineering into a sleek grand touring coupe body. The second-generation Prelude's fuel injection was derived from F1. Then the third gen got four-wheel steering way back in the late '80s. The fourth gen? If you just want to be told today's word, you can jump to the bottom of this article for today's Wordle solution revealed. But if you'd rather solve it yourself, keep reading for some clues, tips, and strategies to assist you. SEE ALSO: Mahjong, Sudoku, free crossword, and more: Play games on Mashable SEE ALSO: NYT Connections hints today: Clues, answers for July 22, 2026 Where did Wordle come from? Originally created by engineer Josh Wardle as a gift for his partner, Wordle rapidly spread to become an international phenomenon, with thousands of people around the globe playing every day. Alternate Wordle versions created by fans also sprang up, including battle royale Squabble , music identification game Heardle , and variations like Dordle and Quordle that make you guess multiple words at once . Wordle eventually became so popular that it was purchased by the New York Times , and TikTok creators even livestream themselves playing . What's the best Wordle starting word? The best Wordle starting word is the one that speaks to you. But if you prefer to be strategic in your approach, we have a few ideas to help you pick a word that might help you find the solution faster. One tip is to select a word that includes at least two different vowels, plus some common consonants like S, T, R, or N. What happened to the Wordle archive? The entire archive of past Wordle puzzles was originally available for anyone to enjoy whenever they felt like it, but it was later taken down , with the website's creator stating it

title: Buy $15 of Takis and score $8 towards a Spider-Man: Brand New Day ticket — everything you need to know
body: TL;DR: Ahead of Spider-Man: Brand New Day 's July 31 theatrical debut, Takis has launched limited-edition collectible bags inspired by the film. From July 1 through Aug. 31, shoppers who spend $15 on qualifying Takis products can earn $8 in Fandango Rewards for a movie ticket. Fans counting down to Spider-Man: Brand New Day can earn a discount on their movie ticket with their next snack run. Takis has launched limited-edition bags inspired by the upcoming Marvel film, and shoppers who spend $15 on participating products through Aug. 31 will receive $8 in Fandango Rewards that can be used towards a ticket to see Spider-Man: Brand New Day . It s not quite web-slinging, but saving $8 on a movie ticket is a pretty good superpower for a bag of chips.

title: The Daily Show reacts to Trump slapping additional tariffs on Canada
body: The word "tariff" seems to have been blissfully absent from the news cycle for a while now, but this week it made a comeback. In the Daily Show clip above, host Desi Lydic reacts to Trump announcing 50 percent tariffs on several goods imported from Canada , from dairy products and paper to furniture and hockey sticks. "No hockey sticks? What's my husband gonna keep jammed in the corner of our garage for the past 15 years?" Lydic said. "Now, obviously, it's very frustrating to be back in this trade war again, but if you're worried that this is another case of Trump abusing his power again relax, these tariffs are legal under the Smoot-Hawley act, which has been around since 1930 when it helped prolong and worse the great depression. So cheer up!" Lydic goes on to explain why the tariffs are being imposed now, citing a Truth Social post in which Trump accuses Canada of "not properly maintaining their forests," leading to the US "being unnecessarily invaded by filthy, polluted, and unhealthy air." "Are you sure you want America to set a precedent for paying for pollution?" asks Lydic. "I feel like your pals at Pesticide-Microplastic-Death-Diarrhea-Lettuce Inc. might have other ideas."

body: Recommendations for the best TVs to buy in 2026 are at the bottom of this guide, thanks to intel from CNET's Ty Pendlebury and PCMag's Will Greenwald . (CNET, PCMag, and Mashable are all owned by publisher Ziff Davis.) Use the table of contents to skip down to a specific category. Buying a new television can break your brain more than it breaks your budget. If you're not a picky cinephile or longtime hardcore gamer , TV specs are one of those Michael Scott "Explain it to me like I'm five" things. Laptop specs are in the same club. Wordy product titles on Amazon TVs definitely aren't helping the confusion. The clusters of nebulous descriptors like "Omni Mini-LED, QLED 4K UHD, Dolby Vision IQ, 144hz, Ambient Experience" aren't exactly accessible to the average person they're just pure chaos. Familiarizing yourself with all of this technical stuff doesn't require a lab or any prior knowledge at all. It might just take a straightforward TV buyers guide, broken down by someone who's used to feeling overwhelmed by tech jargon. So, here's your brain workout for the day: Let's translate all of these TV acronyms and buzzwords so that you know exactly what to look for when buying a new TV. title: What happened on the JoJo Siwa Cruise? Fans describe the weekend at sea
body: For three days in June, JoJo Siwa fans could do more than watch her perform. They could go on vacation with her. The JoJo Siwa Cruise departed from Port Canaveral, Florida, on June 12 for a three-night sailing aboard Royal Caribbean's Utopia of the Seas, where a regular cruise itinerary became a floating fan weekend: rhinestone bow decorating, a dance class, nightly parties hosted by Siwa, a cabana beach day, and a concert at sea. Siwa, the singer, dancer, internet personality, and former Dance Moms star, first became famous as a child performer known for her oversized bows, high-energy music, and brightly colored brand. In the years since, she has grown up in public, moving from children's entertainment into a more adult pop era that has included new music, reality TV appearances, and a fanbase that now includes both younger viewers and adults who have followed her for years. The cruise reflected that broad fanbase. A group of fans who self-identify on Instagram as "The Divas" says the people in their group who attended range in age from 9 to 49. They became bigger fans of Siwa through Celebrity Big Brother and saw the cruise promoted on her social media. "There were so many events and activities," the group tells Mashable, listing bow decorating, dance class, hibachi dinner, a cabana beach day, nightly parties, and the concert. "The concert show she performed too, which was just phenomenal." "She'd worked on new choreography especially for the weekend, so it was really exciting to experience that," Alice, a 32-year-old fan who runs the Joelle Fans UK account, says. View t

title: How to delete your AdultFriendFinder account
body: There are so many reasons to delete an online dating profile. It might be because you've found that special somebody already and you want to show commitment by officially leaving the dating landscape. Perhaps you're just happy to remain as a singleton . Or maybe you just want a break from all swipes, matches, and "heys." Thankfully, AdultFriendFinder is one of the hookup platforms that make it super easy to delete your account and permanently scrub your profile data. First, understand that deleting your account is different from canceling your Gold membership . If you want to continue using AdultFriendFinder for free, or just want to stop your monthly payments, you can just cancel the subscription without deleting your entire account. Actual account deletion is a more permanent action: you ll lose all of your profile info, all of your chat history, and all of your settings. So, be sure before you go ahead consider whether you 100 percent want to wipe all that information. Particularly if you're a longtime user with a lot of history on the site. SEE ALSO: The best sexting apps for discreet NSFW chats If you do want to go ahead, you can choose whether to delete your account via a web browser or on your phone. The steps are different, but the end result is the same. title: Substack adds tool that detects AI-generated content
body: Substack is introducing a new tool to help readers figure out whether content was written by a human or generated by AI. Announced in a blog post on Tuesday, the self-publishing platform's new feature is powered by AI detection tool Pangram , and is available on Substack posts, notes, replies, and comments. SEE ALSO: Substack gives creators control of the conversation with new Reply Rules "Not everything made with AI is slop, and not all slop is made with AI," wrote Substack co-founder and CEO Chris Best. "The problem is when there is a mismatch between a reader s expectation and reality, especially when they unwittingly invest their attention in something with no human thought on the other end." Substack's AI detection tool is available now on web and in the iOS app, with Android to follow. To scan Substack content for AI contributions, simply click or tap on the "..." meatball menu, then select "Scan for AI text." A popup will then appear stating what percentage of the text Pangram has determined is AI-generated, AI-assisted, or human. Substack's new AI detection feature does have some restrictions. Specifically, it will only work on Substack text passages longer than 100 words, and only those published after 8:30 a.m. PT / 11:30 a.m. on July 21. Attempting to scan Substack content published prior to this results in a popup stating that it's ineligible for AI detection. Trying to check passages that are shorter than 100 words also surfaces a popup explaining that there isn't enough text for accurate AI detection. The popup on Best's own posts also offers an additional mes

title: Hurdle hints and answers for July 22, 2026
body: If you like playing daily word games like Wordle , then Hurdle is a great game to add to your routine. There are five rounds to the game. The first round sees you trying to guess the word, with correct, misplaced, and incorrect letters shown in each guess. If you guess the correct answer, it'll take you to the next hurdle, providing the answer to the last hurdle as your first guess. This can give you several clues or none, depending on the words. For the final hurdle, every correct answer from previous hurdles is shown, with correct and misplaced letters clearly shown. An important note is that the number of times a letter is highlighted from previous guesses does necessarily indicate the number of times that letter appears in the final hurdle. title: NYT Pips hints, answers for July 22, 2026
body: Welcome to your guide to Pips, the latest game in the New York Times catalogue. Released in August 2025, Pips puts a unique spin on dominoes, creating a fun single-player experience that could become your next daily gaming habit. Currently, if you're stuck, the game only offers to reveal the entire puzzle, forcing you to move on to the next difficulty level and start over. However, we have you covered! Below are piecemeal answers that will serve as hints so that you can find your way through each difficulty level. How to play Pips If you've ever played dominoes, you'll have a passing familiarity with how Pips is played. As we've shared in our previous hints stories for Pips, the tiles, like dominoes, are placed vertically or horizontally and connect with each other. The main difference between a traditional game of dominoes and Pips is the color-coded conditions you have to address. The touching tiles don't necessarily have to match. SEE ALSO: Wordle today: Answer, hints for July 22, 2026 The conditions you have to meet are specific to the color-coded spaces. For example, if it provides a single number, every side of a tile in that space must add up to the number provided. It is possible and common for only half a tile to be within a color-coded space. Here are common examples you'll run into across the difficulty levels: Number : All the pips in this space must add up to the number. Equal : Every domino half in this space must be the same number of pips. Not Equal : Every domino half in this space must have a completely different number of pips. Less than : Every domino ha

Words can be made from linked letters up, down, left, right, or diagonal, but words can also change direction, resulting in quirky shapes and patterns. title: Range Rover answers the question: "What if we built a not-SUV?"
author: Jonathan M. Gitlin
body: Range Rover provided flights from Washington, DC, to London and accommodation so Ars could see the RR GT, as well as some other things you can read about in early September. Ars does not accept paid editorial content. WOLVERHAMPTON, England—One can argue about whether or not Range Rover invented the luxury SUV category, but there's no question that since the first Range Rover went on sale in 1970, the brand has made itself the standard that other luxury SUVs have to measure against. But what if Range Rover built something that wasn't an SUV? That's exactly what it's doing with the new Range Rover GT. Spy shots have been circulating for at least a year of what was believed to be an all-electric replacement for the Range Rover Velar . It turns out that was a misapprehension; the heavily camouflaged prototypes were examples of the Range Rover GT being tested at the Nürburgring and the Arctic Circle . Will this be Range Rover's answer to the Lucid Air and Porsche Taycan? Credit: Range Rover Range Rover still isn't ready to reveal an uncamouflaged GT, but the car you see is much less disguised than the boxy-looking prototypes seen in earlier spy shots. Read full article Comments

title: Trump administration to spend $1 billion on new border surveillance system
body: More than a thousand new surveillance towers will be erected along U.S. borders, according to a recent Department of Homeland Security (DHS) assessment by the Government Accounting Office. The expanded autonomous surveillance plan , including tech upgrades to existing towers overseen by DHS, is estimated to cost around $1 billion and aims to be finished by 2034. Upon completion, the country's borders will boast 2,300 surveillance towers it currently operates around 830, the Electronic Frontier Foundation reports. SEE ALSO: Google's AI Search is a minefield for schools The expenditure is part of the Customs and Border Protection's (CBP) consolidated Integrated Surveillance Tower (IST) program, which operates a network of intel-gathering towers and surveillance that detect "items of interest" along the border and in nearby towns. The towers are integrated into a central system accessible by agents. "It s not just a barrier. A lot of people miss that [it] has technology built into it," said CBP Commissioner Rodney Scott during an April budget hearing with lawmakers regarding the agency's plans. Building on years of work to outfit border regions with technologically-advanced security systems, the White House has proposed millions in infrastructure for a "smart" border wall , including autonomous and AI-powered surveillance towers that alert agents to potential security threats. In 2025 and 2026, more than $240 million was allocated for the IST program. The 2027 budget steered another $97 million toward the construction of 95 new towers over the next year.

title: On the run for 20 years, most-wanted fugitive caught hiding as a biotech exec
author: Beth Mole
body: One of Rhode Island's most-wanted fugitives was arrested in New York last week after being on the run for more than 20 years, which was at least partially spent hiding as a biotech executive, according to Stat News . In a Securities and Exchange Commission filing Monday, California-based Immix Biopharma noted that Richard Graydon was terminated Friday for " reasons unrelated to his activities at the Company ." It turns out, Richard Graydon was an alias for Ronald Fischer, a 70-year-old former anesthesiologist licensed in Rhode Island and Massachusetts, who fled amid his 2005 trial for first-degree sexual assault. Fischer was accused of raping a woman during a date on his yacht, Lion King . Read full article Comments
